purpose: The 32-hour Tunyan diurnal mapped hour-by-hour. Eight named watches of 4 hours each. Per-hour civic events: meals, services opening/closing, sanitation, post, market activity. Used by NPC schedulers, lens activity feeds, and the day-phase-aware quest engine.

watches.name: Dominus
watches.tone: the new diurnal opens; sanitation finishes its overnight; the city rises

watches.name: Stratus
watches.tone: trade and post open; first meal at the start of the watch's tail

watches.name: Freeus
watches.tone: the productive heart of the diurnal; restaurants and theaters cycle; mid-diurnal meal at hour 11

watches.name: Quartus
watches.tone: post sorted; tea time; museums and art centers open; the adult center opens at the watch's tail

watches.name: Penanus
watches.tone: evening; civic culture peaks; theaters and amphitheaters in their evening run

watches.name: Easeus
watches.tone: supper; the diurnal eases into hearth time; restaurants close to non-night-trade

watches.name: Tendus
watches.tone: rest; city quieter; only the sanitation overnight, the night-watch governance, and the Medici Resonance Choir's quiet hours work through this

watches.name: Toolus
watches.tone: the deepest moon; bakers begin first meal preparation toward the close; the watch hands off to Dominus

notes: The 8 watch names (Dominus / Stratus / Freeus / Quartus / Penanus / Easeus / Tendus / Toolus) come from the source bible image directly. Each watch is exactly 4 hours; the watches are anchored to clock-hours rather than to sun/moon (the sun/moon split varies by month per calendar.json — Wound has 20 sun hours, T'Annum has 12).

Civic culture is dense for a pre-industrial society. Theaters, art centers, museums, amphitheaters, didactic centers, and adult centers all open and close on tracked schedules. Tunyan cities take culture seriously.
